short_description.value: RingMoGPT is a unified multimodal remote sensing foundation model integrating vision, language, and localization tasks. It features a location- and instruction-aware Q-Former and a change detection module, trained on large-scale high-quality image-text and instruction-tuning datasets, supporting tasks such as scene classification, object detection, VQA, image captioning, grounded captioning, and change captioning.

backbone.value: ViT-g/14 (from EVA-CLIP) as visual encoder, Vicuna-13B as LLM

pretext_training_type.value: Adapter-based domain adaptation with frozen visual encoder and LLM, training only adapter parameters

masking_strategy.value: Masked image modeling and instruction-based masking

pretraining.value: Pretrained on 522,769 high-quality RS image-text pairs with rule-based and VLM-generated captions, followed by instruction-tuning on 1.6M QA pairs across six tasks.

value.value: Integration of Q-Former with location and semantic queries
Graph Attention Networks in cross-attention module

dataset.value: Large-scale RS image-text pretraining dataset (522,769 pairs)

sampling.value: Random and category-balanced sampling from public RS datasets

loss_function.value: Image-grounded text generation, contrastive, and matching losses

application.value: Change captioning in bi-temporal RS images
